title: Nigeria Application Form for Visa/Entry Permit (Form Imm. 22)
description: Apply for a Nigerian visa/entry permit through the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS)'s standard "Form Imm. 22 — Application Form for Visa/Entry Permit", scoped to embassy/consular use abroad, opening Nigeria's Visa vertical (3 of 6, alongside Business Formation and Taxes; continuing the Nigeria research thread from GOV-2551/GOV-2553). This specimen is confirmed live at three independently operated mirrors as of 2026-07-12, two of them directly re-fetched this cycle: the Embassy of Nigeria in Vietnam's own government domain (nigeriaembassy.org.vn, HTTP 200, application/pdf, 373,872 bytes, matching the issue's own citation exactly) — a simplified 2-page, 25-numbered-question edition, used as this schema's primary citable `source` since it is the only own-government-domain copy — and a Nigerian-Embassy-Washington-DC-letterheaded copy hosted on a third-party visa-services domain (samspassport.com, HTTP 200, application/pdf, 43,156 bytes), a fuller 3-page, 31-numbered-question edition that itself prints the form's official designation "Form Imm. 22" and is used here as the base for field coverage per this cycle's scope. A third cited mirror (nigerianvisaservices.com) was also directly re-fetched this cycle and returned a live `application/pdf` response (88,509 bytes) on every attempt; it is not byte-identical to the DC/Washington copy (a separately re-typeset rendering, sha256 differs), but independent text extraction confirms it carries the same "Form Imm. 22" content and item numbering, corroborating the DC/Washington edition as NIS's stable standard distributed across missions rather than a single embassy's one-off variant. Both directly-verified editions are flat, print-and-hand-fill specimens (0 AcroForm widgets), completed by hand and mailed to a Nigerian diplomatic mission together with supporting documents and a money-order processing fee. Independent extraction of both editions' full printed text found the two are NOT a strict superset/subset pair as initially characterized: the fuller DC/Imm.22 edition indeed adds substantial content the simplified Vietnam edition omits (number-of-entries, funds available, employment/spouse-in-Nigeria particulars, prior Nigerian-visa history, immigration/criminal/health-history screening, countries lived-in/visited in the last 5 years and 12 months, and the fee/attachments schedule), but the Vietnam edition also independently asks 6 items the DC edition omits entirely (e-mail address, complexion, academic qualifications, source of sponsorship, and a mission-localized "reference in [country]" name/address/telephone). This schema models the union: the DC/Imm.22 edition's 31 numbered items supply the base structure (86 of this schema's 93 `fields[]` entries, including three bounded repeating tables — a 3-slot prior-Nigeria-visit table and two 4-slot countries-lived-in/visited tables, per this registry's established bounded-repeating-group convention), plus the Vietnam edition's 6 edition-unique items (modelled as independently optional fields, since a specimen's omission of a question this registry has directly verified another edition asks is not evidence the question is inapplicable). 9 `documents[]` entries capture the photograph requirement (printed on both editions), the DC edition's page-3 attachments-and-fee schedule (original passport, letter of invitation, prepaid return mailer, an online visa-fee payment, a $20 money-order processing fee, an optional $65 expedited-service fee), its under-16 accompanying-child document bundle, and its wet-ink certification statement. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Nigeria, the Nigeria Immigration Service, or any Nigerian diplomatic mission.

source.documentRef: Form Imm. 22 — "Application Form for Visa/Entry Permit" (Nigeria Immigration Service), 2-page flat (0 AcroForm widgets) PDF as distributed by the Embassy of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in Vietnam, cross-referenced against a fuller 3-page edition distributed by the Embassy of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in Washington, DC for field-coverage completeness (see `verification.notes` and VERIFICATION.md).

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2561, continuing Nigeria's research thread from GOV-2551 (parent research issue) and GOV-2553 (Taxes, 2/6). Independently re-fetched both citable mirrors directly: nigeriaembassy.org.vn (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 373,872 bytes, Last-Modified Sat 21 Nov 2020, matching the issue's own citation exactly) and samspassport.com (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 43,156 bytes, Last-Modified Tue 29 Oct 2019, 3 pages, 31 numbered questions matching the issue's description, and printing the form's own designation "Form Imm. 22" at its header). The third cited mirror (nigerianvisaservices.com) was independently re-fetched on a later pass this same cycle (both with and without a browser-like User-Agent) and returned a live `application/pdf` response (88,509 bytes, sha256 8b60aceaaa963eb8ad9690e74613b5cf9118baf6a94af60eb4989b127a2ba544, Last-Modified Sat 30 Sep 2017) each time — the earlier Cloudflare-challenge observation is disclosed in VERIFICATION.md as a transient failure, not a genuine access gate. Its content is not byte-identical to the DC/Washington copy (a separately re-typeset rendering with different character spacing, consistent with OCR/re-layout rather than a raw file copy), but a fresh pdfjs-dist text extraction confirms it carries the same "Form Imm. 22" title, item numbering, and full item text as the DC/Washington copy, corroborating it as a third independent distribution of the same underlying NIS form rather than an unrelated document. pdfjs-dist (legacy build, v3) getAnnotations() confirms 0 Widget annotations across both directly-verified editions' pages — flat, print-and-hand-fill forms, consistent with this registry's rw/dgie/visa-application precedent. getTextContent() (position-sorted by transform x/y) was used to read each edition's full printed text directly. Independent side-by-side comparison of the two editions' numbered items found the issue's characterization of the DC edition as a strict superset of the Vietnam edition does not hold exactly: while the DC edition (31 items) does add substantial content the Vietnam edition (25 items) omits, the Vietnam edition also independently asks 6 items the DC edition omits entirely (e-mail address, complexion, academic qualifications, source of sponsorship, and a mission-localized reference-in-country name/address/telephone) — corrected and disclosed in VERIFICATION.md rather than silently adopting the issue's framing. This schema models the union, using the DC edition's 31-item structure (which prints the form's own "Form Imm. 22" designation) as the base for 86 of 93 fields[] entries, plus the Vietnam edition's 6 edition-unique items as independently optional fields. Conformance run: 2 valid mock scenarios (0 errors each) and 5 mutation-control fixtures (exactly 1 error each) using a disposable validate_instance.mjs script (not committed) implementing this schema's own required/requiredWhen grammar plus ajv for the static JSON Schema subset — see VERIFICATION.md for the full write-up.

fields.name: otherNames
fields.label: Other Names
fields.description: Given/first name(s), as distinguished from the surname on the same numbered row.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 1: "Other Names"; Vietnam edition item 1: "FIRST NAME"

fields.name: formerName
fields.label: Former name (if any)
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition. Not required: an applicant with no former name has nothing to declare and neither edition provides a separate "none" checkbox.
fields.sourceRef: Vietnam edition item 1 continuation: "FORMER NAME (if any)"

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sex
fields.description: Neither edition prints more than the binary Male/Female option anywhere on the specimen.

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital Status
fields.description: The DC edition prints only the label "Marital Status" with a blank; the enum boundary is taken from the Vietnam edition's more explicit instruction, which spells out the same question's options.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 2: "Marital Status"; Vietnam edition item 5: "MARITIAL STATUS (Single, Married, Widowed, Divorced)"

fields.name: previousNationality
fields.label: Previous Nationality
fields.description: Not required: an applicant with no former nationality has nothing to declare here.

fields.name: residentialAddress
fields.label: Residential Address
fields.description: Printed as a two-line blank on the source; modelled as a single free-text field.

fields.name: officeAddress
fields.label: Office Address
fields.description: Not required: an applicant without an office (e.g. unemployed, student, retired) has nothing to declare here.

fields.name: armedForcesService
fields.label: Branches of the Armed Forces served or serving in
fields.description: Not required: an applicant with no military service has nothing to declare here.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 8: "State what branches of the Armed Forces of your country you have served or are serving in"

fields.name: armedForcesFrom
fields.label: Armed Forces service: From
fields.description: Not machine-gated on armedForcesService since that sibling field is itself optional and genuinely absent for a non-applicable applicant.

fields.name: complexion
fields.label: Complexion
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition's equivalent item 9 row.

fields.name: visibleIdentificationMarks
fields.label: Visible Identification marks
fields.description: Not required: an applicant with none has nothing to declare and neither edition provides a separate "none" checkbox.

fields.name: purposeOfJourney
fields.label: Purpose of journey
fields.description: The source asks for "full details".
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 12: "Purpose of journey (please give full details)"

fields.name: numberOfEntriesRequired
fields.label: Number of entries required
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 13: "Number of entries required" — "A. Single" / "B. Multiple"

fields.name: modeOfTravelToNigeria
fields.label: Mode of travel to Nigeria
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 15b: "Mode of travel to Nigeria"

fields.name: fundsAvailableForTrip
fields.label: Funds available for the trip
fields.description: Modelled as free text since the source prints no currency instruction beyond "how much money".
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 16: "Indicate how much money is available for your trip"

fields.name: employmentEmployerName
fields.label: Employment reason: Name of Employer(s)
fields.description: Applicable only if the reason for journey (item 12) is employment; not machine-gated since purposeOfJourney is free text with no reliable machine match against "employment".

fields.name: employmentPostToBeOccupied
fields.label: Employment reason: Post to be occupied

fields.name: employmentJobDescription
fields.label: Employment reason: Description of job
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 17c: "Give a full description of job"

fields.name: spouseParentsEmploymentParticulars
fields.label: Employment of spouse/parents in Nigeria
fields.description: Applicable only if joining a spouse, parents, or relatives; not machine-gated (no dedicated eligibility field for this pathway on either edition).
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 18: "Persons joining spouses, parents or relatives, should give full particulars of the employment of parents or spouse in Nigeria including the address where they reside"

fields.name: spouseParentsDurationInNigeria
fields.label: How long has/have your spouse/parents been in Nigeria
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 19: "How long has/have your spouse/parents been in Nigeria"

fields.name: intendedAddressInNigeria
fields.label: Intended address in Nigeria
fields.description: The source explicitly excludes a P.O. Box.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 20: "Your intended address in Nigeria (NOT P.O. BOX)"

fields.name: previousNigerianVisaApplied
fields.label: Have you ever applied for a Nigerian visa before?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 21: "Have you ever applied for a Nigerian visa before? Yes/No"

fields.name: previousVisaApplicationLocation
fields.label: Where did you apply for the visa?
fields.description: Safe to gate on previousNigerianVisaApplied since that field is itself always required/present.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 22: "Where did you apply for the visa?"

fields.name: previousVisaOutcome
fields.label: Was the visa granted or rejected?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 23: "Indicate whether visa was granted or rejected"

fields.name: previousVisaRejectionReason
fields.label: Reason if visa was rejected
fields.description: Gated on previousVisaOutcome equalling 'rejected'; the equals comparison naturally evaluates false (not required) when previousVisaOutcome is itself absent, so this is safe even though the gating field is optional.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 24: "Give reason if visa was rejected"

fields.name: previouslyVisitedNigeria
fields.label: Have you ever visited Nigeria?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 25: "Have you ever visited Nigeria? Yes/No"

fields.name: purposeOfPreviousVisit
fields.label: Purpose of previous visit
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 26: "If yes, was it for:" — "i. Tourism/Visit" / "ii. Business" / "iii. Temporary Employment" / "iv. Residency" / "v. Transit"

fields.name: previousVisit1From
fields.label: Previous visit 1: From
fields.description: DC/Imm.22 item 27 prints a bounded 3-row table (rows i-iii), not an open-ended list; flattened here to previousVisit1..3, matching this registry's bounded-repeating-group convention.

fields.name: yearsLivedInApplyingCountry
fields.label: How long have you lived in the country where you are applying?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 28: "How long have you lived in the country where you are applying for visa/entry permit?"

fields.name: everInfectedContagiousDiseaseOrMentalIllness
fields.label: Ever infected by a contagious disease or suffered serious mental illness?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29a: "Been infected by any contagious disease (e.g. Tuberculosis) or suffered serious mental illness? Yes/No"

fields.name: everArrestedOrConvicted
fields.label: Ever arrested or convicted for any offence or crime?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29b: "Been arrested or convicted for any offence or crime even though subject of pardon, arrested or other such legal action? Yes/No"

fields.name: everInvolvedNarcoticActivities
fields.label: Ever involved in narcotic activities?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29c: "Been involved in narcotic activities? Yes/No"

fields.name: deportedFromCountry
fields.label: Deported from which country?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29d continuation: "if so, from which country?"

fields.name: everSoughtVisaByMisrepresentationOrFraud
fields.label: Ever sought to obtain a visa by misrepresentation or fraud?
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29e: "Sought to obtain a visa by misrepresentation or fraud? Yes/No"

fields.name: everTestedPositiveHivAids
fields.label: Ever tested positive for HIV/AIDS?
fields.description: Faithfully modelled from the source's own printed question per this standard's source-of-truth-fidelity principle, not a GovSchema-invented field.
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22 item 29f: "Tested positive for HIV/AIDS? If yes, please state status"

fields.name: countryLived1Country
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 1: Country
fields.description: DC/Imm.22 item 30 prints a bounded 4-row table, not an open-ended list; flattened here to countryLived1..4, matching this registry's bounded-repeating-group convention. Not required: an applicant who has lived in fewer than 4 countries in the last 5 years leaves the remaining rows blank.

fields.name: countryLived1City
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 1: City

fields.name: countryLived1Date
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 1: Date

fields.name: countryLived2Country
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 2: Country

fields.name: countryLived2City
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 2: City

fields.name: countryLived2Date
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 2: Date

fields.name: countryLived3Country
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 3: Country

fields.name: countryLived3City
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 3: City

fields.name: countryLived3Date
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 3: Date

fields.name: countryLived4Country
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 4: Country

fields.name: countryLived4City
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 4: City

fields.name: countryLived4Date
fields.label: Country lived in (last 5 years), row 4: Date

fields.name: countryVisited1Country
fields.label: Country visited (last 12 months), row 1: Country
fields.description: DC/Imm.22 item 31 prints a second bounded 4-row table, flattened here to countryVisited1..4, matching this registry's bounded-repeating-group convention.

fields.name: emailAddress
fields.label: E-mail address
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition.

fields.name: academicQualifications
fields.label: Academic qualifications
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition.

fields.name: sourceOfSponsorship
fields.label: Source of sponsorship for proposed visit
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition.
fields.sourceRef: Vietnam edition item 25: "SOURCE OF SPONSORSHIP FOR PROPOSED VISIT TO NIGERIA"

fields.name: missionCountryReferenceNameAddress
fields.label: Reference in country of application: Name and Address
fields.description: Vietnam-edition-only field, mission-localized: the Vietnam specimen prints "REFERENCE IN VIETNAM", naming the mission's own host country; other missions' editions of this same item would substitute their own country name. Not printed on the DC/Imm.22 edition.
fields.sourceRef: Vietnam edition item 24: "REFERENCE IN VIETNAM (Name and Address of people who maybe be contacted when necessary)"

fields.name: missionCountryReferenceTelephone
fields.label: Reference in country of application: Telephone No.

fields.name: signatureDate
fields.label: Signature date
fields.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "Date" beside the (unwidgeted) signature line; Vietnam edition, page 2: "DATE" beside the signature line

documents.label: Photograph
documents.sourceRef: Both editions, page 1, right margin: "PHOTOGRAPH"; DC/Imm.22 page 3 attachments list specifies "colored passport photo showing full face on white background"

documents.label: Letter of invitation from Nigeria
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "letter of invitation from Nigeria"

documents.label: Return prepaid mailer (Fedex, DHL, UPS, or Express mail)
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "return prepaid Fedex, DHL, UPS, or Express mail, if applying by mail, DO NOT ATTACH ENVELOPE AND STAMP"

documents.label: Requirements for children under sixteen (birth certificate, 1 passport photograph, signed parental consent letter, copies of parents' passports/valid ID)
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "REQUIREMENTS FOR CHILDREN UNDER SIXTEEN: Copy of birth certificate of child, 1 passport photograph, signed letter of consent by both parents and copies of the parents' passports (bio-data page) or valid ID MUST accompany the application." Applicable only when the applicant is under 16; not machine-gated (this schema has no dedicated applicant-is-a-minor eligibility field).

documents.label: Online visa fee payment
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "VISA Fees are to be paid through the Nigeria immigration Website at WWW.immigration.gov.ng."

documents.label: Processing fee (money order)
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "Once paid, print out the form and mail to the Embassy with a PROCESSING FEE of $20.00 in money order made out to the Embassy of Nigeria."

documents.label: Expedited service fee (money order)
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3: "If expedited service is required, an additional fee of $65.00 in money order made out to the Nigerian Embassy should be attached."

documents.label: Certification of compliance with immigration laws
documents.statement: I understand that I will be required to comply with the Immigration/Alien and other laws governing entry of Immigration into the country for which I now apply for Visa/Entry Permit.
documents.sourceRef: DC/Imm.22, page 3, beside the (unwidgeted) signature line. The Vietnam edition prints an equivalent but differently worded statement: "I hereby undertake to comply with the immigration and other laws governing the entry of immigrants into Nigeria to which I now apply for entry visa / permit."
